title: sambucus cerulea var. glauca
intro: Sambucus cerulea var. glauca, also known as blue elderberry, is a deciduous shrub or small tree in the family Adoxaceae, native to the Pacific coast of North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties, making it effective against colds, flu, and other respiratory infections. The health benefits of blue elderberry include reducing inflammation and improving immune function, as well as providing antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. Blue elderberry can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and capsules, which can be used to support immune function and overall health.
